---
title: "BSS-GH Weather & Tide Covariate Analysis and Integration"
author: "Matt George"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = TRUE, warning = TRUE)
```

```{r}
###############################################################################
# Crab Creel Estimation, Westport / Grays Harbor
# Weather & Tide Covariate Module
# Version: 0.2.3 (2026-06-20). See BSS-GH-pooled-CPUE-weather-tide-covariates-documentation.md
#          Section 9 for the change history.
#          v0.2.3 inherits pooled-model v6.9 (B1.7, single-cell hierarchical scale
#          collapsed in the shared augmented Stan model) and wraps each component's
#          fit + LOO in a tryCatch so one failing component no longer aborts the
#          whole module run.
#          v0.2.2 brings the BSS engine to pooled-model v6.8 parity (B1.5
#          neg-binomial effort marginalization, B1.6 unconditional sigma_IE
#          prior), adds a fixed RNG seed for reproducible baseline/covariate/CV
#          fits, per-fit model diagnostics (structural params, divergence
#          localization, posterior predictive calibration), and session capture.
#          The module's LOO-reliability convergence gate is unchanged; the pooled
#          model's distortion-aware gate does not apply here because LOO is more
#          sensitive to divergences than the summed totals.
#          v0.2.0 brings the BSS engine to pooled-model v6.5 parity (divergence
#          gate, boat sampler tuning, per-population AR cap) and fixes the
#          interview schema mismatch (departure-time proxy for completed trips).
#
# PURPOSE
#   Test hypotheses about tide and weather effects on recreational Dungeness
#   crab effort and CPUE; integrate supported covariates into the BSS model
#   via linear predictors on mu_E and mu_C.
#
# HYPOTHESES TESTED
#   H1  Daily boat effort varies with tide phase during daylight hours, after
#       accounting for weekend/holiday and weather. Tested via (a) trailer
#       count ~ tide features and (b) fraction of interview departures that
#       fall on rising/flood tide vs. uniform expectation.
#   H2  Daily shore CPUE varies with tide features (range, daytime high-tide
#       hour, etc.) after accounting for weekend/holiday and weather.
#   H3  Boat effort and CPUE depend on sea state (wind, wave height) due to
#       bar-crossing constraints at Grays Harbor.
#
# DESIGN
#   Layer B  Daily-level GAM screening of candidate covariates.
#   Layer C  Covariate-augmented BSS vs baseline BSS; compare via PSIS-LOO
#            with k-fold time-block CV fallback when Pareto-k diagnostics
#            are unreliable.
#
# RUNS ANNUALLY
#   All parameters key off est_date_start / est_date_end inherited from the
#   main model run. Tide/weather data are cached locally to avoid repeated
#   API calls. Station availability is tested at runtime with automatic
#   failover to backup stations ranked by proximity.
#
# DEPENDENCIES
#   Must be run AFTER BSS-GH-pooled-CPUE-model.Rmd (uses same interview
#   and effort pipelines). The covariate fits use the augmented Stan model
#   crab_bss_pooled_weather_adjusted.stan, which adds covariate blocks to the
#   pooled model and collapses to the baseline when K_E = K_C = 0.
###############################################################################

# ===========================================================================
# 0. SETUP
# ===========================================================================

load.lib <- c("tidyverse","lubridate","suncalc","mgcv","loo","rstan","here",
              "readxl","httr","jsonlite","geosphere","patchwork")
install.lib <- load.lib[!load.lib %in% installed.packages()]
for(lib in install.lib) install.packages(lib, dependencies = TRUE)
sapply(load.lib, require, character = TRUE)
rstan_options(auto_write = TRUE)

# Null-coalescing operator (base R >= 4.4 provides this natively; define it if
# absent so the module is robust on older R and when run standalone).
if(!exists("%||%", mode = "function")) `%||%` <- function(a, b) if(is.null(a)) b else a

# Source shared functions if present (matches main RMD structure)
if(dir.exists(here("03_R_functions"))) {
  purrr::walk(list.files(here("03_R_functions"), full.names = TRUE), source)
}

run_date   <- format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d")
output_dir <- here("05_output", run_date, "pooled-CPUE-covariates")
cache_dir  <- here("cache", "weather_tide")
for(d in c(output_dir, cache_dir)) if(!dir.exists(d)) dir.create(d, recursive = TRUE)

# ===========================================================================
# 0.1 PARAMETERS
# These mirror the main model so the covariate run uses the same date window,
# populations, and sub-seasons. Add tide/weather specific parameters.
# ===========================================================================

params <- list(
  # --- Inherit from main model ---
  project_name      = "Coastal Recreational Crab",
  fishery_name      = "Rec Crab Grays Harbor 2024-25",
  est_date_start    = "2024-09-16",
  est_date_end      = "2025-09-15",
  season_filter     = "2024-25",
  pot_open_date     = "2024-12-01",
  days_wkend        = c("Friday","Saturday","Sunday"),
  min_fishing_time  = 0.5,
  period_pe         = "week",
  sections          = c(1),

  # --- BSS pass-through ---
  # Both the baseline and covariate fits use the augmented Stan model below.
  # The baseline is obtained by zeroing the covariate blocks (K_E = K_C = 0),
  # not by loading crab_bss_pooled.stan, so that the baseline and covariate
  # log_lik are produced by the same model and stay comparable for PSIS-LOO.
  bss_model_file_covariates  = "crab_bss_pooled_weather_adjusted.stan",
  bss_chains                 = 4,
  bss_iter_default           = 3000,
  bss_warmup_default         = 1500,
  bss_iter_shore_allgear     = 5000,
  bss_warmup_shore_allgear   = 2500,
  bss_adapt_delta            = 0.9,
  bss_cores                  = 4,
  bss_max_treedepth_default  = 10,
  bss_min_interviews         = 20,
  bss_max_count_seq          = 3,
  ar_daily_min_coverage      = 0.25,
  ar_daily_min_obs           = 20,
  ar_weekly_min_per_week     = 1.5,

  # --- v6.x BSS parity (ported from BSS-GH-pooled-CPUE-model.Rmd) -----------
  # The module fits its own BSS, so the convergence machinery the main model
  # gained in v6.1-v6.5 must be mirrored here, or the boat fit diverges and the
  # baseline-vs-covariate LOO comparison is untrustworthy.
  # v6.5 per-population AR resolution cap: the boat trailer-count series cannot
  # identify a daily AR(1), so it is capped at weekly (cuts the latent AR
  # dimension ~7x). Shore stays at daily, where it converges.
  ar_max_resolution          = list(shore = "daily", private_boat = "weekly"),
  boat_use_gear_hours        = TRUE,
  # v6.2 per-fit sampler tuning. Boat all-gear needs adapt_delta 0.99 /
  # treedepth 13 (it diverged ~100% at the defaults); shore all-gear 14 / 0.95;
  # ring-net 12 / 0.95; everything else the defaults (10 / 0.9).
  bss_iter_boat_allgear      = 5000,
  bss_warmup_boat_allgear    = 2500,
  bss_treedepth_boat_allgear = 13,
  bss_delta_boat_allgear     = 0.99,
  bss_treedepth_shore_allgear= 14,
  bss_delta_shore_allgear    = 0.95,
  bss_treedepth_ringnet      = 12,
  bss_delta_ringnet          = 0.95,
  # v6.1 divergence gate: a fit with more than this many divergent transitions
  # is flagged as non-converged, so its LOO elpd is treated as unreliable.
  max_divergences            = 5,

  # B1.6: fixed RNG seed for reproducible baseline, covariate, and CV fits.
  bss_seed                   = 20260619,

  # --- Departure-time proxy (boat departure-on-flood hypothesis) ------------
  # The crab interview export records interview_time and hours_fished but no
  # fishing start time. With this TRUE, departure is approximated as
  # interview_time - hours_fished for completed trips, which assumes the
  # interview is taken at trip end (a returning crabber at the access point).
  # Set FALSE to skip the interview-derived departure-tide features entirely;
  # the daily tide/weather covariates and the GAM screens are unaffected either
  # way. The rigorous alternative is interval-level I/E departure data.
  use_interview_departure_proxy = TRUE,

  # --- Tide stations (ordered by priority; proximity-ranked backups) ---
  # Coordinates are approximate CO-OPS station locations. The fetch routine
  # probes metadata at runtime to confirm status.
  tide_stations = tibble::tribble(
    ~id,        ~name,             ~lat,      ~lon,       ~priority,
    "9441102",  "Westport, WA",    46.9043,   -124.1051,   1,
    "9440910",  "Toke Point, WA",  46.7076,   -123.9672,   2,
    "9442396",  "La Push, WA",     47.9133,   -124.6367,   3
  ),
  tide_datum      = "MLLW",
  tide_units      = "metric",
  tide_interval   = "h",   # hourly. "6" = 6-min if available and needed.
  tide_timezone   = "lst_ldt",  # local standard + daylight saving; avoid GMT surprises

  # --- NDBC buoys (offshore wind/wave) ---
  ndbc_buoys = tibble::tribble(
    ~id,      ~name,                  ~lat,     ~lon,     ~priority,
    "46211",  "Grays Harbor",          46.857,  -124.244,   1,
    "46029",  "Columbia River Bar",    46.144,  -124.510,   2,
    "46041",  "Cape Elizabeth, WA",    47.353,  -124.731,   3,
    "46087",  "Neah Bay",              48.494,  -124.728,   4
  ),

  # --- ASOS land stations ---
  asos_stations = tibble::tribble(
    ~id,      ~name,                ~lat,      ~lon,      ~priority,
    "KHQM",   "Hoquiam/Bowerman",    46.9728,  -123.9364,   1,
    "KAST",   "Astoria, OR",         46.1581,  -123.8831,   2,
    "KOLM",   "Olympia, WA",         46.9691,  -122.9027,   3
  ),

  # --- GSOD fallback station codes ---
  gsod_stations = c("727923-24227", "727910-24227"),  # KHQM, KAST

  # --- Caching ---
  cache_tide   = TRUE,
  cache_ndbc   = TRUE,
  cache_asos   = TRUE,
  force_refetch = FALSE,

  # --- Exploratory GAM thresholds (Layer B screen) ---
  gam_p_threshold    = 0.10,  # loose at screen; LOO confirms at BSS step
  gam_min_edf_smooth = 1.5,   # min effective df to consider a smooth non-linear
  vif_max            = 5.0,   # collinearity cap among covariates at the same level

  # --- BSS covariate inclusion rule ---
  cov_elpd_margin_in_se = 4,    # dELPD > 4*SE to include
  cov_prior_sd          = 0.35, # normal prior SD on gamma (log scale)

  # --- LOO diagnostics ---
  loo_k_threshold      = 0.7,
  loo_k_pct_trigger    = 0.10,  # if >10% have k>0.7, trigger k-fold fallback
  cv_folds             = 5,

  # --- Project location (for sun times) ---
  centroid_lat = 46.904,
  centroid_lon = -124.105,
  local_tz     = "America/Los_Angeles"
)

# Crabbing holidays: read from the single-source workbook (04_input_files/crabbing_holidays.xlsx)
# via 03_R_functions/read_crabbing_holidays.R, the same source the main models use, instead of a
# hardcoded copy. The shared prep_days_crab() reads holidays from params, so set it there. (In an
# orchestrated run params is inherited from the pooled model, which already carries season_filter
# and crabbing_holidays_file.)
params$crabbing_holiday_dates <- read_crabbing_holidays(params)
crabbing_holiday_dates <- params$crabbing_holiday_dates

subseason_1 <- list(name = "ring_net_only",
                    start = as.Date(params$est_date_start),
                    end   = as.Date(params$pot_open_date) - 1)
subseason_2 <- list(name = "all_gear",
                    start = as.Date(params$pot_open_date),
                    end   = as.Date(params$est_date_end))

cat(sprintf("Covariate run window: %s to %s (%d days)\n",
            params$est_date_start, params$est_date_end,
            as.integer(as.Date(params$est_date_end) - as.Date(params$est_date_start) + 1)))


# ===========================================================================
# 1. NOAA CO-OPS TIDE DATA — availability check + chunked fetch + fallback
#
# CO-OPS API docs: https://api.tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov/api/prod/
# Metadata API:    https://api.tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov/mdapi/prod/
#
# Limits:
#   - "water_level" (verified observations): max 31 days per request
#   - "predictions": max 365 days per request, but hourly is fine
#
# Strategy:
#   - Try verified observations first (actual measurements, most accurate)
#   - If gaps, fill with predictions from same station
#   - If entire station unavailable, fall through to next station by priority
# ===========================================================================

coops_station_metadata <- function(station_id) {
  url <- sprintf("https://api.tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov/mdapi/prod/webapi/stations/%s.json",
                 station_id)
  resp <- tryCatch(httr::GET(url, httr::timeout(15)), error = function(e) NULL)
  if(is.null(resp) || httr::status_code(resp) != 200) return(NULL)
  content <- tryCatch(jsonlite::fromJSON(httr::content(resp, "text", encoding = "UTF-8"),
                                          simplifyDataFrame = FALSE),
                      error = function(e) NULL)
  if(is.null(content)) return(NULL)
  content$stations
}

coops_fetch_chunk <- function(station_id, begin_date, end_date, product,
                              datum = "MLLW", units = "metric",
                              time_zone = "lst_ldt", interval = NULL) {
  # CO-OPS API: dates as YYYYMMDD in URL
  url <- "https://api.tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov/api/prod/datagetter"
  query <- list(
    product      = product,
    application  = "WDFW_creel",
    begin_date   = format(as.Date(begin_date), "%Y%m%d"),
    end_date     = format(as.Date(end_date), "%Y%m%d"),
    datum        = datum,
    station      = station_id,
    time_zone    = time_zone,
    units        = units,
    format       = "json"
  )
  if(!is.null(interval)) query$interval <- interval

  resp <- tryCatch(httr::GET(url, query = query, httr::timeout(30)),
                   error = function(e) NULL)
  if(is.null(resp) || httr::status_code(resp) != 200) {
    return(tibble(t = as.POSIXct(character()), v = numeric(), station = character(),
                  product = character(), error = TRUE))
  }
  txt <- httr::content(resp, "text", encoding = "UTF-8")
  js  <- tryCatch(jsonlite::fromJSON(txt), error = function(e) NULL)
  if(is.null(js)) {
    return(tibble(t = as.POSIXct(character()), v = numeric(), station = character(),
                  product = character(), error = TRUE))
  }
  # API returns either $data (success) or $error (failure)
  if(!is.null(js$error)) {
    return(tibble(t = as.POSIXct(character()), v = numeric(), station = character(),
                  product = character(), error = TRUE))
  }
  dat <- as_tibble(js$data)
  if(nrow(dat) == 0) {
    return(tibble(t = as.POSIXct(character()), v = numeric(), station = character(),
                  product = character(), error = FALSE))
  }
  dat <- dat |>
    mutate(
      t       = as.POSIXct(t, tz = params$local_tz, format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M"),
      v       = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(v)),
      station = station_id,
      product = product,
      error   = FALSE
    ) |>
    filter(!is.na(v)) |>
    select(t, v, station, product, error)
  dat
}

coops_fetch_range <- function(station_id, begin_date, end_date,
                              product = "water_level",
                              chunk_days = 30) {
  # Break into 30-day chunks for verified water_level; predictions can go longer
  begin_date <- as.Date(begin_date); end_date <- as.Date(end_date)
  chunks <- seq(begin_date, end_date, by = paste(chunk_days, "days"))
  if(tail(chunks, 1) < end_date) chunks <- c(chunks, end_date)
  starts <- head(chunks, -1); ends <- pmin(tail(chunks, -1), end_date)
  # ensure pairs cover full range exactly once
  starts[1] <- begin_date

  interval <- if(product == "predictions") "h" else NULL  # hourly predictions
  out <- purrr::map2_dfr(starts, ends, function(s, e) {
    Sys.sleep(0.1)  # be polite to the API
    coops_fetch_chunk(station_id, s, e, product = product, interval = interval,
                      datum = params$tide_datum, units = params$tide_units,
                      time_zone = params$tide_timezone)
  })
  out
}

fetch_tide_with_fallback <- function(stations_df, begin_date, end_date,
                                     cache_dir = NULL, force_refetch = FALSE) {
  # Ordered by priority; loop until we have a complete time series
  cache_key <- sprintf("tide_%s_%s.rds", as.Date(begin_date), as.Date(end_date))
  cache_file <- file.path(cache_dir, cache_key)
  if(!force_refetch && !is.null(cache_dir) && file.exists(cache_file)) {
    cat(sprintf("  Tide: loading from cache %s\n", basename(cache_file)))
    return(readRDS(cache_file))
  }

  stations_df <- stations_df |> arrange(priority)
  all_obs <- tibble()
  station_log <- tibble()

  for(i in seq_len(nrow(stations_df))) {
    st <- stations_df[i, ]
    cat(sprintf("  Trying CO-OPS station %s (%s)...\n", st$id, st$name))

    meta <- coops_station_metadata(st$id)
    if(is.null(meta)) {
      station_log <- bind_rows(station_log, tibble(station = st$id, status = "metadata_unavailable"))
      next
    }

    # Fetch verified observations
    obs <- coops_fetch_range(st$id, begin_date, end_date, product = "water_level")
    if(nrow(obs) > 0 && !any(obs$error)) {
      obs$source <- "verified"
      all_obs <- bind_rows(all_obs, obs)
      station_log <- bind_rows(station_log,
                               tibble(station = st$id, status = "verified_ok",
                                      n_obs = nrow(obs)))

      # Expected hourly obs for this window
      expected_n <- as.integer(as.Date(end_date) - as.Date(begin_date) + 1) * 24
      coverage <- nrow(obs) / expected_n
      cat(sprintf("    Got %d verified hourly obs (%.0f%% of expected)\n",
                  nrow(obs), coverage * 100))
      if(coverage > 0.9) break  # good enough
    } else {
      station_log <- bind_rows(station_log, tibble(station = st$id, status = "verified_failed"))
    }
  }

  # If still have gaps, supplement with predictions from primary station
  if(nrow(all_obs) == 0) {
    # Fall back entirely to predictions from primary
    st <- stations_df[1, ]
    cat(sprintf("  All verified fetches failed. Using predictions from %s.\n", st$id))
    preds <- coops_fetch_range(st$id, begin_date, end_date, product = "predictions")
    preds$source <- "prediction"
    all_obs <- preds
  } else {
    # Fill gaps: any hour missing from verified -> add prediction
    have_hours <- all_obs |>
      mutate(hour_key = format(t, "%Y-%m-%d %H:00")) |>
      pull(hour_key) |> unique()
    all_hours <- format(seq(as.POSIXct(begin_date, tz = params$local_tz),
                            as.POSIXct(paste(end_date, "23:00"), tz = params$local_tz),
                            by = "hour"), "%Y-%m-%d %H:00")
    missing_n <- sum(!all_hours %in% have_hours)
    if(missing_n > 0) {
      cat(sprintf("  Filling %d missing hours with predictions...\n", missing_n))
      st <- stations_df[1, ]
      preds <- coops_fetch_range(st$id, begin_date, end_date, product = "predictions")
      if(nrow(preds) > 0) {
        preds <- preds |>
          mutate(hour_key = format(t, "%Y-%m-%d %H:00")) |>
          filter(!hour_key %in% have_hours) |>
          select(-hour_key) |>
          mutate(source = "prediction")
        all_obs <- bind_rows(all_obs, preds) |> arrange(t)
      }
    }
  }

  result <- list(obs = all_obs, station_log = station_log)
  if(!is.null(cache_dir)) saveRDS(result, cache_file)
  result
}


# ===========================================================================
# 2. NDBC BUOY DATA — offshore wind and wave
#
# NDBC serves fixed-width text files by buoy and year.
# URL: https://www.ndbc.noaa.gov/view_text_file.php?filename={id}h{year}.txt.gz&dir=data/historical/stdmet/
# For the current year (still active): https://www.ndbc.noaa.gov/data/realtime2/{id}.txt
# ===========================================================================

ndbc_fetch_historical <- function(buoy_id, year) {
  url <- sprintf("https://www.ndbc.noaa.gov/view_text_file.php?filename=%sh%d.txt.gz&dir=data/historical/stdmet/",
                 tolower(buoy_id), year)
  resp <- tryCatch(httr::GET(url, httr::timeout(30)), error = function(e) NULL)
  if(is.null(resp) || httr::status_code(resp) != 200) return(NULL)
  txt <- httr::content(resp, "text", encoding = "UTF-8")
  if(nchar(txt) < 100 || grepl("not found", tolower(txt))) return(NULL)

  # NDBC format: two header rows (names and units), then space-separated data
  lines <- strsplit(txt, "\n", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]
  if(length(lines) < 3) return(NULL)
  hdr <- strsplit(sub("^#", "", lines[1]), "\\s+")[[1]]
  hdr <- hdr[nchar(hdr) > 0]

  body <- paste(lines[3:length(lines)], collapse = "\n")
  df <- tryCatch(read.table(textConnection(body), header = FALSE,
                             col.names = hdr, stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
                             na.strings = c("MM", "99", "999", "9999")),
                 error = function(e) NULL)
  if(is.null(df)) return(NULL)

  # Columns can be YY MM DD hh mm or #YY MM DD hh mm; normalize
  time_cols <- intersect(c("YY","YYYY","MM","DD","hh","mm"), names(df))
  if(all(c("MM","DD","hh","mm") %in% names(df))) {
    yr_col <- if("YYYY" %in% names(df)) df$YYYY else df$YY
    # Older format uses YY (2-digit), newer uses YYYY (4-digit)
    if(max(yr_col, na.rm = TRUE) < 100) yr_col <- yr_col + 1900L + (yr_col < 50) * 100L
    df$t <- as.POSIXct(sprintf("%04d-%02d-%02d %02d:%02d",
                               yr_col, df$MM, df$DD, df$hh, df$mm),
                       tz = "UTC")
    df$t <- with_tz(df$t, params$local_tz)
  }

  # Standardized fields we care about; column names vary year-to-year
  df |>
    mutate(
      wind_dir_deg = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[[first(intersect(c("WDIR","WD"), names(df)))]])),
      wind_ms      = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[[first(intersect(c("WSPD"), names(df)))]])),
      gust_ms      = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[[first(intersect(c("GST"), names(df)))]])),
      wave_h_m     = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[[first(intersect(c("WVHT"), names(df)))]])),
      dom_pd_s     = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[[first(intersect(c("DPD"), names(df)))]])),
      avg_pd_s     = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[[first(intersect(c("APD"), names(df)))]])),
      mean_dir_deg = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[[first(intersect(c("MWD"), names(df)))]])),
      air_temp_c   = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[[first(intersect(c("ATMP"), names(df)))]])),
      sst_c        = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[[first(intersect(c("WTMP"), names(df)))]])),
      pressure_hpa = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[[first(intersect(c("PRES"), names(df)))]]))
    ) |>
    select(t, any_of(c("wind_dir_deg","wind_ms","gust_ms","wave_h_m","dom_pd_s",
                       "avg_pd_s","mean_dir_deg","air_temp_c","sst_c","pressure_hpa"))) |>
    mutate(buoy = buoy_id) |>
    filter(!is.na(t))
}

ndbc_fetch_realtime <- function(buoy_id) {
  url <- sprintf("https://www.ndbc.noaa.gov/data/realtime2/%s.txt", toupper(buoy_id))
  resp <- tryCatch(httr::GET(url, httr::timeout(20)), error = function(e) NULL)
  if(is.null(resp) || httr::status_code(resp) != 200) return(NULL)
  txt <- httr::content(resp, "text", encoding = "UTF-8")
  lines <- strsplit(txt, "\n", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]
  if(length(lines) < 3) return(NULL)
  hdr <- strsplit(sub("^#", "", lines[1]), "\\s+")[[1]]
  hdr <- hdr[nchar(hdr) > 0]
  body <- paste(lines[3:length(lines)], collapse = "\n")
  df <- tryCatch(read.table(textConnection(body), header = FALSE,
                             col.names = hdr, stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
                             na.strings = c("MM", "99", "999", "9999")),
                 error = function(e) NULL)
  if(is.null(df) || nrow(df) == 0) return(NULL)
  if(all(c("MM","DD","hh","mm") %in% names(df))) {
    yr <- df$YY
    if(max(yr, na.rm = TRUE) < 100) yr <- yr + 2000L
    df$t <- with_tz(as.POSIXct(sprintf("%04d-%02d-%02d %02d:%02d",
                                       yr, df$MM, df$DD, df$hh, df$mm), tz = "UTC"),
                    params$local_tz)
  }
  df |>
    transmute(
      t,
      wind_dir_deg = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[["WDIR"]])),
      wind_ms      = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[["WSPD"]])),
      gust_ms      = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[["GST"]])),
      wave_h_m     = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[["WVHT"]])),
      air_temp_c   = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[["ATMP"]])),
      sst_c        = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[["WTMP"]])),
      pressure_hpa = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[["PRES"]])),
      buoy         = buoy_id
    ) |>
    filter(!is.na(t))
}

fetch_buoy_with_fallback <- function(buoys_df, begin_date, end_date,
                                     cache_dir = NULL, force_refetch = FALSE) {
  cache_file <- file.path(cache_dir, sprintf("ndbc_%s_%s.rds", begin_date, end_date))
  if(!force_refetch && !is.null(cache_dir) && file.exists(cache_file)) {
    cat(sprintf("  NDBC: loading from cache %s\n", basename(cache_file)))
    return(readRDS(cache_file))
  }

  buoys_df <- buoys_df |> arrange(priority)
  years <- year(as.Date(begin_date)):year(as.Date(end_date))
  current_year <- year(Sys.Date())

  all_obs <- tibble()
  station_log <- tibble()

  for(i in seq_len(nrow(buoys_df))) {
    bu <- buoys_df[i, ]
    cat(sprintf("  Trying NDBC buoy %s (%s)...\n", bu$id, bu$name))
    buoy_obs <- tibble()

    for(yr in years) {
      if(yr < current_year) {
        d <- ndbc_fetch_historical(bu$id, yr)
        if(!is.null(d) && nrow(d) > 0) buoy_obs <- bind_rows(buoy_obs, d)
      } else {
        # Realtime feed for current/recent data
        d <- ndbc_fetch_realtime(bu$id)
        if(!is.null(d) && nrow(d) > 0) buoy_obs <- bind_rows(buoy_obs, d)
      }
    }

    if(nrow(buoy_obs) > 0) {
      buoy_obs <- buoy_obs |> filter(t >= as.POSIXct(begin_date, tz = params$local_tz),
                                      t <= as.POSIXct(paste(end_date, "23:59"),
                                                       tz = params$local_tz))
    }

    if(nrow(buoy_obs) == 0) {
      station_log <- bind_rows(station_log,
                               tibble(buoy = bu$id, status = "unavailable", n_obs = 0))
      next
    }

    station_log <- bind_rows(station_log,
                             tibble(buoy = bu$id, status = "ok", n_obs = nrow(buoy_obs)))
    # Assess coverage; if under 50%, keep trying next buoy
    expected_n <- as.integer(as.Date(end_date) - as.Date(begin_date) + 1) * 24
    coverage <- nrow(buoy_obs) / expected_n
    cat(sprintf("    %d obs (%.0f%% of expected)\n", nrow(buoy_obs), coverage * 100))

    all_obs <- bind_rows(all_obs, buoy_obs)
    if(coverage > 0.5) break  # primary buoy has acceptable coverage
  }

  result <- list(obs = all_obs, station_log = station_log)
  if(!is.null(cache_dir)) saveRDS(result, cache_file)
  result
}


# ===========================================================================
# 3. ASOS / GSOD LAND STATION WEATHER
#
# ASOS via Iowa State IEM (free, public): https://mesonet.agron.iastate.edu/
#   Endpoint: https://mesonet.agron.iastate.edu/cgi-bin/request/asos.py?...
#
# GSOD via NCEI: https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/services/data/v1
#   Fallback: daily summaries only; sufficient if sub-hourly ASOS fails.
# ===========================================================================

iem_asos_fetch <- function(station_id, begin_date, end_date) {
  url <- "https://mesonet.agron.iastate.edu/cgi-bin/request/asos.py"
  query <- list(
    station = toupper(station_id),
    data    = "tmpc,dwpc,sknt,gust,drct,p01m,vsby,skyc1",
    year1   = year(as.Date(begin_date)),  month1 = month(as.Date(begin_date)),
    day1    = day(as.Date(begin_date)),
    year2   = year(as.Date(end_date)),    month2 = month(as.Date(end_date)),
    day2    = day(as.Date(end_date)),
    tz      = "Etc/UTC",
    format  = "onlycomma",
    latlon  = "no",
    missing = "M",
    trace   = "T",
    direct  = "no",
    report_type = 3
  )
  resp <- tryCatch(httr::GET(url, query = query, httr::timeout(60)),
                   error = function(e) NULL)
  if(is.null(resp) || httr::status_code(resp) != 200) return(NULL)
  txt <- httr::content(resp, "text", encoding = "UTF-8")
  df <- tryCatch(read.csv(textConnection(txt), na.strings = c("M","")),
                 error = function(e) NULL)
  if(is.null(df) || nrow(df) == 0) return(NULL)

  df |>
    transmute(
      t           = with_tz(as.POSIXct(valid, tz = "UTC"), params$local_tz),
      temp_c      = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(tmpc)),
      dewpt_c     = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(dwpc)),
      wind_ms     = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(sknt)) * 0.514444,  # knots to m/s
      gust_ms     = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(gust)) * 0.514444,
      wind_dir_deg = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(drct)),
      precip_mm   = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(p01m)),
      visibility_mi = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(vsby)),
      station_id  = station_id
    ) |>
    filter(!is.na(t))
}

gsod_fetch <- function(station_id, begin_date, end_date) {
  # NCEI v1 data access. GSOD station IDs are typically "USAF-WBAN" (e.g., 727923-24227)
  url <- "https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/services/data/v1"
  query <- list(
    dataset    = "global-summary-of-the-day",
    stations   = station_id,
    startDate  = format(as.Date(begin_date), "%Y-%m-%d"),
    endDate    = format(as.Date(end_date), "%Y-%m-%d"),
    format     = "json",
    units      = "metric"
  )
  resp <- tryCatch(httr::GET(url, query = query, httr::timeout(60)),
                   error = function(e) NULL)
  if(is.null(resp) || httr::status_code(resp) != 200) return(NULL)
  txt <- httr::content(resp, "text", encoding = "UTF-8")
  js  <- tryCatch(jsonlite::fromJSON(txt), error = function(e) NULL)
  if(is.null(js) || length(js) == 0) return(NULL)
  as_tibble(js) |>
    mutate(
      date = as.Date(DATE),
      temp_c_mean = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(TEMP)),
      wind_ms_mean = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(WDSP)) * 0.514444,
      wind_ms_max  = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(MXSPD)) * 0.514444,
      gust_ms_max  = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(GUST))  * 0.514444,
      precip_mm    = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(PRCP)) * 25.4,  # inches to mm
      station_id   = station_id
    ) |>
    select(any_of(c("date","temp_c_mean","wind_ms_mean","wind_ms_max",
                    "gust_ms_max","precip_mm","station_id")))
}

fetch_asos_with_fallback <- function(asos_df, gsod_fallback_ids,
                                     begin_date, end_date,
                                     cache_dir = NULL, force_refetch = FALSE) {
  cache_file <- file.path(cache_dir, sprintf("asos_%s_%s.rds", begin_date, end_date))
  if(!force_refetch && !is.null(cache_dir) && file.exists(cache_file)) {
    cat(sprintf("  ASOS: loading from cache %s\n", basename(cache_file)))
    return(readRDS(cache_file))
  }

  asos_df <- asos_df |> arrange(priority)
  station_log <- tibble()
  hourly <- tibble()

  for(i in seq_len(nrow(asos_df))) {
    st <- asos_df[i, ]
    cat(sprintf("  Trying ASOS %s (%s)...\n", st$id, st$name))
    d <- iem_asos_fetch(st$id, begin_date, end_date)
    if(!is.null(d) && nrow(d) > 0) {
      hourly <- d
      station_log <- bind_rows(station_log,
                               tibble(station = st$id, status = "ok_asos", n_obs = nrow(d)))
      break
    } else {
      station_log <- bind_rows(station_log,
                               tibble(station = st$id, status = "failed_asos", n_obs = 0))
    }
  }

  daily_gsod <- tibble()
  if(nrow(hourly) == 0) {
    # GSOD fallback
    for(sid in gsod_fallback_ids) {
      cat(sprintf("  Trying GSOD %s...\n", sid))
      d <- gsod_fetch(sid, begin_date, end_date)
      if(!is.null(d) && nrow(d) > 0) {
        daily_gsod <- d
        station_log <- bind_rows(station_log,
                                 tibble(station = sid, status = "ok_gsod", n_obs = nrow(d)))
        break
      } else {
        station_log <- bind_rows(station_log,
                                 tibble(station = sid, status = "failed_gsod", n_obs = 0))
      }
    }
  }

  result <- list(hourly = hourly, daily_gsod = daily_gsod, station_log = station_log)
  if(!is.null(cache_dir)) saveRDS(result, cache_file)
  result
}


# ===========================================================================
# 4. DAILY COVARIATE CONSTRUCTION
#
# Inputs: hourly tide obs, hourly buoy met, hourly ASOS met (or daily GSOD)
# Output: one row per date with named covariates.
# ===========================================================================

build_sun_calendar <- function(dates, lat, lon, tz) {
  sun <- suncalc::getSunlightTimes(date = as.Date(dates), lat = lat, lon = lon, tz = tz)
  tibble(
    event_date     = as.Date(dates),
    dawn           = as.POSIXct(sun$dawn, tz = tz),
    dusk           = as.POSIXct(sun$dusk, tz = tz),
    daylight_hours = as.numeric(difftime(sun$dusk, sun$dawn, units = "hours"))
  )
}

# ---- Tide daily features ----
# Strategy: identify local max/min (high/low tide) per day; derive features.
# Daytime is "between dawn and dusk."
#
# Features (all daily):
#   tide_max_m            Max tide height (m) over 24h
#   tide_min_m            Min tide height (m) over 24h
#   tide_range_m          tide_max_m - tide_min_m (spring-neap proxy)
#   daytime_tide_max_m
#   daytime_tide_min_m
#   daytime_tide_range_m
#   daytime_high_hour     Hour of the maximum tide within daylight
#   n_high_tides_daytime  Count of local maxima during daylight
#   flood_hours_daytime   Hours during daylight when tide is rising
#   tidal_energy          sqrt(sum of squared dh/dt), proxy for current energy
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
compute_tide_daily_features <- function(tide_obs, sun_cal) {
  if(nrow(tide_obs) == 0) {
    warning("No tide observations; returning NA features.")
    return(sun_cal |>
             mutate(tide_max_m = NA_real_, tide_min_m = NA_real_, tide_range_m = NA_real_,
                    daytime_tide_max_m = NA_real_, daytime_tide_min_m = NA_real_,
                    daytime_tide_range_m = NA_real_, daytime_high_hour = NA_real_,
                    n_high_tides_daytime = NA_real_, flood_hours_daytime = NA_real_,
                    tidal_energy = NA_real_) |>
             select(-dawn, -dusk, -daylight_hours))
  }

  td <- tide_obs |>
    mutate(
      event_date = as.Date(format(t, "%Y-%m-%d")),
      hour = hour(t) + minute(t) / 60
    ) |>
    arrange(t)

  # Full-day extrema
  daily_full <- td |>
    group_by(event_date) |>
    summarise(
      tide_max_m = max(v, na.rm = TRUE),
      tide_min_m = min(v, na.rm = TRUE),
      tide_range_m = tide_max_m - tide_min_m,
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  # Daytime subset
  td_with_sun <- td |> left_join(sun_cal, by = "event_date") |>
    mutate(in_daylight = t >= dawn & t <= dusk)
  daytime <- td_with_sun |> filter(in_daylight) |>
    group_by(event_date) |>
    summarise(
      daytime_tide_max_m = if(n() > 0) max(v, na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_,
      daytime_tide_min_m = if(n() > 0) min(v, na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_,
      daytime_tide_range_m = daytime_tide_max_m - daytime_tide_min_m,
      daytime_high_hour = hour[which.max(v)],
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  # Local maxima and flood (rising) hours during daylight
  flood_flags <- td_with_sun |>
    arrange(event_date, t) |>
    group_by(event_date) |>
    mutate(
      dv = c(NA_real_, diff(v)),
      is_rising = dv > 0
    ) |>
    ungroup()

  flood_daytime <- flood_flags |> filter(in_daylight, !is.na(is_rising)) |>
    group_by(event_date) |>
    summarise(
      flood_hours_daytime = sum(is_rising),  # assuming hourly sampling
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  # Detect local maxima via sign change in dv; count daytime peaks
  peaks <- flood_flags |>
    group_by(event_date) |>
    mutate(
      next_dv = lead(dv),
      is_peak = (dv > 0) & (!is.na(next_dv) & next_dv < 0)
    ) |>
    filter(in_daylight, is_peak) |>
    summarise(n_high_tides_daytime = n(), .groups = "drop")

  # Tidal energy: sqrt(sum dv^2)
  energy <- flood_flags |>
    group_by(event_date) |>
    summarise(tidal_energy = sqrt(sum(dv^2, na.rm = TRUE)), .groups = "drop")

  sun_cal |>
    left_join(daily_full, by = "event_date") |>
    left_join(daytime, by = "event_date") |>
    left_join(flood_daytime, by = "event_date") |>
    left_join(peaks, by = "event_date") |>
    left_join(energy, by = "event_date") |>
    mutate(
      n_high_tides_daytime = replace_na(n_high_tides_daytime, 0L),
      flood_hours_daytime = replace_na(flood_hours_daytime, NA_real_)
    ) |>
    select(-dawn, -dusk, -daylight_hours)
}

# ---- Weather daily features ----
# Prefers NDBC (offshore) for wave/wind, supplements ASOS for temp/precip.
# If only daily GSOD is available, uses its precomputed daily stats.
compute_weather_daily_features <- function(buoy_obs, asos_hourly, daily_gsod, sun_cal) {
  buoy_daily <- tibble(event_date = sun_cal$event_date,
                       wind_ms_buoy_mean = NA_real_, gust_ms_buoy_max = NA_real_,
                       wave_h_m_mean = NA_real_, wave_h_m_max = NA_real_,
                       sst_c_mean = NA_real_, pressure_hpa_mean = NA_real_)
  if(nrow(buoy_obs) > 0) {
    buoy_daily <- buoy_obs |>
      mutate(event_date = as.Date(format(t, "%Y-%m-%d"))) |>
      group_by(event_date) |>
      summarise(
        wind_ms_buoy_mean = mean(wind_ms, na.rm = TRUE),
        gust_ms_buoy_max  = max(gust_ms, na.rm = TRUE),
        wave_h_m_mean     = mean(wave_h_m, na.rm = TRUE),
        wave_h_m_max      = max(wave_h_m, na.rm = TRUE),
        sst_c_mean        = mean(sst_c, na.rm = TRUE),
        pressure_hpa_mean = mean(pressure_hpa, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
      )
  }

  asos_daily <- tibble(event_date = sun_cal$event_date,
                       temp_c_min = NA_real_, temp_c_max = NA_real_,
                       precip_mm_total = NA_real_,
                       wind_ms_asos_mean = NA_real_,
                       visibility_mi_min = NA_real_)
  if(nrow(asos_hourly) > 0) {
    asos_daily <- asos_hourly |>
      mutate(event_date = as.Date(format(t, "%Y-%m-%d"))) |>
      group_by(event_date) |>
      summarise(
        temp_c_min        = min(temp_c, na.rm = TRUE),
        temp_c_max        = max(temp_c, na.rm = TRUE),
        precip_mm_total   = sum(precip_mm, na.rm = TRUE),
        wind_ms_asos_mean = mean(wind_ms, na.rm = TRUE),
        visibility_mi_min = min(visibility_mi, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
      )
  } else if(nrow(daily_gsod) > 0) {
    asos_daily <- daily_gsod |>
      transmute(event_date = date,
                temp_c_min = NA_real_, temp_c_max = temp_c_mean,  # GSOD gives mean only
                precip_mm_total = precip_mm,
                wind_ms_asos_mean = wind_ms_mean,
                visibility_mi_min = NA_real_)
  }

  sun_cal |>
    select(event_date) |>
    left_join(buoy_daily, by = "event_date") |>
    left_join(asos_daily, by = "event_date") |>
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ifelse(is.infinite(.x), NA_real_, .x)))
}


# ---- Interview-level tide features (for boat departure hypothesis) ----
# For each interviewed boat trip, attach:
#   tide height at fishing_start_time (departure)
#   tide phase: "rising" or "falling" in the hour containing departure
#   Indicator: 1 if departure within ±2h of daytime high tide, else 0
#
# Aggregates to daily covariate: prop_boat_departures_on_flood (or near-high)
# for inclusion as daily BSS covariate.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
compute_interview_tide_features <- function(interview_df, tide_obs) {
  if(nrow(interview_df) == 0 || nrow(tide_obs) == 0) {
    return(list(per_interview = tibble(), daily = tibble()))
  }

  # --- Construct a departure timestamp ---------------------------------------
  # Priority: (1) an explicit fishing_start_time if the schema has one;
  # (2) otherwise a proxy of interview_time - hours_fished for completed trips,
  # which the crab export supports (interview_time is an Excel serial datetime,
  # so its fractional part is the local time of day). The proxy assumes the
  # interview is taken at the end of the trip; it is restricted to completed
  # trips, and any lag between return and interview biases departure later.
  # (3) If neither is available (or the proxy is disabled), return empty so the
  # daily tide/weather covariates and the GAM screens still run.
  use_proxy <- isTRUE(params$use_interview_departure_proxy %||% TRUE)
  has_start <- "fishing_start_time" %in% names(interview_df) &&
               any(!is.na(interview_df$fishing_start_time))
  can_proxy <- use_proxy &&
               all(c("interview_time", "hours_fished") %in% names(interview_df)) &&
               any(!is.na(suppressWarnings(as.numeric(interview_df$interview_time))) &
                   !is.na(interview_df$hours_fished))

  if(has_start) {
    int <- interview_df |>
      filter(!is.na(fishing_start_time)) |>
      mutate(depart_posix = as.POSIXct(paste(event_date, fishing_start_time),
                                       format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M",
                                       tz = params$local_tz)) |>
      filter(!is.na(depart_posix))
  } else if(can_proxy) {
    message("  compute_interview_tide_features: no fishing_start_time; using the ",
            "interview_time - hours_fished departure proxy for completed trips ",
            "(assumes the interview is at trip end). Set ",
            "params$use_interview_departure_proxy = FALSE to disable.")
    int <- interview_df |>
      mutate(interview_time = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(interview_time))) |>
      filter(!is.na(interview_time), !is.na(hours_fished),
             trip_status %in% "Complete") |>
      mutate(
        time_of_day_sec = (interview_time - floor(interview_time)) * 86400,
        depart_posix = as.POSIXct(paste(event_date, "00:00:00"), tz = params$local_tz) +
                       time_of_day_sec - hours_fished * 3600
      ) |>
      filter(!is.na(depart_posix))
  } else {
    message("  compute_interview_tide_features: no fishing_start_time and no usable ",
            "interview_time/hours_fished (or proxy disabled); skipping departure-tide ",
            "features. Daily tide/weather covariates and the GAM screens are unaffected.")
    return(list(per_interview = tibble(), daily = tibble()))
  }

  if(nrow(int) == 0) return(list(per_interview = tibble(), daily = tibble()))

  # Attach nearest hourly tide obs and compute phase from neighboring values
  tide_sorted <- tide_obs |> arrange(t)

  attach_tide <- function(tstamps) {
    # For each tstamp find nearest tide obs by time
    idx <- findInterval(as.numeric(tstamps), as.numeric(tide_sorted$t))
    idx <- pmax(pmin(idx, nrow(tide_sorted) - 1), 1)
    h_at <- tide_sorted$v[idx]
    h_next <- tide_sorted$v[idx + 1]
    rising <- (h_next > h_at)
    tibble(tide_height_at_depart = h_at, tide_rising_at_depart = rising)
  }

  tide_feats <- attach_tide(int$depart_posix)
  per_int <- bind_cols(int, tide_feats)

  # Near-high-tide indicator: within ±2h of nearest local max in daytime
  sun_cal <- build_sun_calendar(unique(per_int$event_date),
                                 params$centroid_lat, params$centroid_lon,
                                 params$local_tz)
  td_with_sun <- tide_sorted |>
    mutate(event_date = as.Date(format(t, "%Y-%m-%d"))) |>
    left_join(sun_cal, by = "event_date") |>
    mutate(in_daylight = t >= dawn & t <= dusk)

  peaks <- td_with_sun |>
    arrange(event_date, t) |>
    group_by(event_date) |>
    mutate(dv = c(NA_real_, diff(v)),
           next_dv = lead(dv),
           is_peak = (dv > 0) & (!is.na(next_dv) & next_dv < 0)) |>
    filter(in_daylight, is_peak) |>
    ungroup() |>
    select(event_date, peak_t = t)

  per_int <- per_int |>
    left_join(peaks |> group_by(event_date) |>
                summarise(peak_times = list(peak_t), .groups = "drop"),
              by = "event_date") |>
    mutate(
      near_high = purrr::map2_lgl(depart_posix, peak_times, function(dt, pts) {
        if(is.null(pts) || length(pts) == 0) return(NA)
        any(abs(as.numeric(difftime(dt, pts, units = "hours"))) <= 2)
      })
    )

  daily <- per_int |>
    filter(population == "private_boat") |>
    group_by(event_date) |>
    summarise(
      n_boat_interviews = n(),
      prop_depart_on_flood = mean(tide_rising_at_depart, na.rm = TRUE),
      prop_depart_near_high = mean(near_high, na.rm = TRUE),
      mean_depart_tide_height_m = mean(tide_height_at_depart, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  list(per_interview = per_int |>
         select(interview_id, event_date, population, depart_posix,
                tide_height_at_depart, tide_rising_at_depart, near_high),
       daily = daily)
}


# ===========================================================================
# 4.5 BOAT DEPARTURE CLUSTERING TEST
# Tests whether boat departure times cluster non-uniformly around tide phase,
# independently of effort modeling. Null: uniform distribution across the
# daytime tidal cycle. This is the direct test of the reformulated H1.
# ===========================================================================

departure_clustering_test <- function(per_interview_df, tide_obs, sun_cal) {
  if(nrow(per_interview_df) == 0) return(list(test = NULL))
  boat <- per_interview_df |> filter(population == "private_boat")
  if(nrow(boat) < 20) {
    return(list(test = NULL, note = sprintf("Only %d boat interviews; test skipped.",
                                            nrow(boat))))
  }

  # Observed count of departures on flood/rising
  n <- nrow(boat)
  k_flood <- sum(boat$tide_rising_at_depart, na.rm = TRUE)

  # Expected flood fraction (in daytime): rough estimate = 0.5 by symmetry,
  # BUT daytime tide phase is not uniform within the calendar. Estimate the
  # true expected flood fraction during actual fishable hours from the tide
  # record itself.
  tide_daytime <- tide_obs |>
    mutate(event_date = as.Date(format(t, "%Y-%m-%d"))) |>
    left_join(sun_cal, by = "event_date") |>
    filter(t >= dawn & t <= dusk) |>
    arrange(t) |>
    mutate(dv = c(NA_real_, diff(v))) |>
    filter(!is.na(dv))
  expected_flood_fraction <- mean(tide_daytime$dv > 0, na.rm = TRUE)

  # One-sided binomial test
  bt <- binom.test(k_flood, n, p = expected_flood_fraction, alternative = "greater")

  tibble(
    n_boat_interviews = n,
    k_departures_rising = k_flood,
    observed_fraction = k_flood / n,
    expected_fraction_given_daylight = expected_flood_fraction,
    binomial_p_value = bt$p.value,
    binomial_ci_lo = bt$conf.int[1],
    binomial_ci_hi = bt$conf.int[2]
  )
}


# ===========================================================================
# 5. LAYER B  —  Daily GAM screen
#
# Purpose: rapidly identify which daily tide/weather covariates carry
# signal beyond day-of-year seasonality and weekend/holiday indicators.
# GAMs provide a flexible non-parametric fit that avoids imposing a
# functional form; smooth terms with edf near 1 that are significant
# suggest near-linear effects suitable for the BSS log-linear predictor.
#
# Outputs:
#   effort_gam, cpue_gam         fitted mgcv::gam objects
#   effort_selected_features    vector of candidate covariates to carry
#                                forward into BSS for effort
#   cpue_selected_features       same for CPUE
# ===========================================================================

gam_screen_effort <- function(effort_daily, covariate_df, population) {
  merged <- effort_daily |>
    inner_join(covariate_df, by = "event_date") |>
    mutate(
      yday = yday(event_date),
      is_weekend = as.integer(weekdays(event_date) %in% params$days_wkend),
      is_holiday = as.integer(event_date %in% crabbing_holiday_dates)
    ) |>
    filter(!is.na(mean_count))

  cat(sprintf("  GAM effort screen (%s): %d daily obs\n", population, nrow(merged)))
  if(nrow(merged) < 30) {
    cat("    Insufficient data for GAM; skipping.\n")
    return(list(model = NULL, selected = character()))
  }

  # Candidate covariates for effort
  candidates <- c("tide_range_m","daytime_tide_range_m","daytime_high_hour",
                  "n_high_tides_daytime","flood_hours_daytime","tidal_energy",
                  "wind_ms_buoy_mean","gust_ms_buoy_max","wave_h_m_mean","wave_h_m_max",
                  "precip_mm_total","temp_c_max",
                  "prop_depart_on_flood","prop_depart_near_high",
                  "mean_depart_tide_height_m")
  available <- intersect(candidates, names(merged))
  # Drop near-constant or mostly NA covariates
  available <- available[sapply(merged[available], function(x)
    sum(!is.na(x)) >= 0.5 * nrow(merged) && sd(x, na.rm = TRUE) > 1e-6)]

  if(length(available) == 0) {
    cat("    No available covariates with sufficient variation.\n")
    return(list(model = NULL, selected = character()))
  }

  # Interview-derived departure covariates only meaningful for boats
  if(population != "private_boat") {
    available <- setdiff(available, c("prop_depart_on_flood",
                                      "prop_depart_near_high",
                                      "mean_depart_tide_height_m"))
  }

  smooth_terms <- paste0("s(", available, ", k = 5)")
  rhs <- paste(c("is_weekend", "is_holiday", "s(yday, k = 8)", smooth_terms),
               collapse = " + ")

  formula <- as.formula(paste("mean_count ~", rhs))

  fit <- tryCatch(
    mgcv::gam(formula, family = quasipoisson(link = "log"), data = merged,
              method = "REML", select = TRUE),
    error = function(e) {cat(sprintf("    GAM error: %s\n", e$message)); NULL}
  )

  if(is.null(fit)) return(list(model = NULL, selected = character()))

  s_tab <- as_tibble(summary(fit)$s.table, rownames = "term")
  p_tab <- as_tibble(summary(fit)$p.table, rownames = "term")

  keep <- s_tab |>
    mutate(var = gsub("s\\(|,.*|\\)","", term)) |>
    filter(var %in% available, `p-value` < params$gam_p_threshold)

  selected <- keep$var
  cat(sprintf("    Covariates passing screen: %s\n",
              if(length(selected) > 0) paste(selected, collapse = ", ") else "none"))

  list(model = fit, summary_smooth = s_tab, summary_param = p_tab,
       selected = selected, data = merged)
}

gam_screen_cpue <- function(interview_df, covariate_df, population) {
  if(nrow(interview_df) == 0) return(list(model = NULL, selected = character()))

  hrs_col <- if(population == "private_boat") "gear_time_total" else "fishing_time_total"
  daily <- interview_df |>
    filter(!is.na(.data[[hrs_col]]), .data[[hrs_col]] > 0, dungeness_kept >= 0) |>
    group_by(event_date) |>
    summarise(
      catch = sum(dungeness_kept, na.rm = TRUE),
      hours = sum(.data[[hrs_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
      n_int = n(),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) |>
    mutate(log_hours = log(hours))

  merged <- daily |>
    inner_join(covariate_df, by = "event_date") |>
    mutate(
      yday = yday(event_date),
      is_weekend = as.integer(weekdays(event_date) %in% params$days_wkend),
      is_holiday = as.integer(event_date %in% crabbing_holiday_dates)
    )

  cat(sprintf("  GAM CPUE screen (%s): %d daily obs\n", population, nrow(merged)))
  if(nrow(merged) < 30) {
    cat("    Insufficient data for GAM; skipping.\n")
    return(list(model = NULL, selected = character()))
  }

  # Candidate covariates for CPUE (tide features primary; weather secondary)
  candidates <- c("tide_range_m","daytime_tide_range_m","daytime_high_hour",
                  "n_high_tides_daytime","flood_hours_daytime","tidal_energy",
                  "daytime_tide_max_m","daytime_tide_min_m",
                  "wind_ms_buoy_mean","wave_h_m_mean",
                  "sst_c_mean","pressure_hpa_mean",
                  "precip_mm_total")
  available <- intersect(candidates, names(merged))
  available <- available[sapply(merged[available], function(x)
    sum(!is.na(x)) >= 0.5 * nrow(merged) && sd(x, na.rm = TRUE) > 1e-6)]

  if(length(available) == 0) return(list(model = NULL, selected = character()))

  smooth_terms <- paste0("s(", available, ", k = 5)")
  rhs <- paste(c("is_weekend", "is_holiday", "s(yday, k = 8)", smooth_terms),
               collapse = " + ")
  formula <- as.formula(paste("catch ~ offset(log_hours) +", rhs))

  fit <- tryCatch(
    mgcv::gam(formula, family = quasipoisson(link = "log"), data = merged,
              method = "REML", select = TRUE),
    error = function(e) {cat(sprintf("    GAM error: %s\n", e$message)); NULL}
  )
  if(is.null(fit)) return(list(model = NULL, selected = character()))

  s_tab <- as_tibble(summary(fit)$s.table, rownames = "term")
  p_tab <- as_tibble(summary(fit)$p.table, rownames = "term")

  keep <- s_tab |>
    mutate(var = gsub("s\\(|,.*|\\)","", term)) |>
    filter(var %in% available, `p-value` < params$gam_p_threshold)

  selected <- keep$var
  cat(sprintf("    Covariates passing screen: %s\n",
              if(length(selected) > 0) paste(selected, collapse = ", ") else "none"))

  list(model = fit, summary_smooth = s_tab, summary_param = p_tab,
       selected = selected, data = merged)
}


# ===========================================================================
# 6. COVARIATE MATRIX ASSEMBLY
#
# Build X_E and X_C as centered/standardized matrices, check multicollinearity
# via VIF, drop worst offenders until all VIF < params$vif_max.
# ===========================================================================

build_covariate_matrix <- function(selected_features, covariate_df, date_vec) {
  if(length(selected_features) == 0) {
    return(list(X = matrix(0, nrow = length(date_vec), ncol = 0),
                features = character(), means = numeric(), sds = numeric()))
  }

  df <- tibble(event_date = date_vec) |>
    left_join(covariate_df, by = "event_date")

  X_raw <- as.matrix(df[, selected_features, drop = FALSE])

  # Handle missings by column-mean imputation (transparent and flagged)
  for(j in seq_len(ncol(X_raw))) {
    col <- X_raw[, j]
    if(any(is.na(col))) {
      m <- mean(col, na.rm = TRUE)
      X_raw[is.na(col), j] <- m
      cat(sprintf("    NA-imputed %d obs in %s with mean %.3f\n",
                  sum(is.na(col)), selected_features[j], m))
    }
  }

  means <- colMeans(X_raw)
  sds   <- apply(X_raw, 2, sd)
  sds[sds < 1e-8] <- 1
  X <- sweep(X_raw, 2, means, "-")
  X <- sweep(X, 2, sds, "/")

  # VIF pruning
  if(ncol(X) > 1) {
    repeat {
      # VIF via OLS of each predictor on the others
      vifs <- sapply(seq_len(ncol(X)), function(j) {
        fit <- lm(X[, j] ~ X[, -j, drop = FALSE])
        1 / (1 - summary(fit)$r.squared)
      })
      names(vifs) <- colnames(X)
      max_vif <- max(vifs, na.rm = TRUE)
      if(max_vif <= params$vif_max || ncol(X) <= 1) break
      drop_var <- names(which.max(vifs))
      cat(sprintf("    Dropping %s (VIF = %.1f)\n", drop_var, max_vif))
      keep <- setdiff(colnames(X), drop_var)
      X <- X[, keep, drop = FALSE]
      means <- means[keep]; sds <- sds[keep]
    }
  }

  list(X = X, features = colnames(X), means = means, sds = sds)
}


# ===========================================================================
# 7. BSS AUGMENTED FIT WRAPPER
# Mirrors prep_bss_crab() from the main RMD but adds X_E, X_C to stan_data.
# ===========================================================================

prep_bss_crab_augmented <- function(days, summ, est_catch_group, params, population_name,
                                    ie_data, covariate_df, effort_features, cpue_features) {

  # Replicate base BSS data prep (minimum viable copy of main RMD logic)
  eff <- summ$effort_index |> filter(count_sequence <= params$bss_max_count_seq)
  D <- nrow(days); G <- 1L; S <- 1L
  eff_d <- eff |>
    left_join(days |> select(event_date, day_index), by = "event_date") |>
    filter(!is.na(day_index))

  is_shore <- (population_name == "shore")
  use_gear_hrs <- !is_shore && isTRUE(params$boat_use_gear_hours %||% TRUE)

  n_effort_days <- n_distinct(eff_d$day_index)
  coverage <- n_effort_days / D
  n_weeks <- max(days$week_index)
  obs_per_week <- n_effort_days / n_weeks

  # Data-driven finest resolution, then v6.5 per-population cap (mirrors the
  # main RMD). The boat trailer-count series is too weakly informative for a
  # daily AR(1), so it is capped at weekly.
  if(coverage >= params$ar_daily_min_coverage && n_effort_days >= params$ar_daily_min_obs) {
    ar_resolution <- "daily"
  } else if(obs_per_week >= params$ar_weekly_min_per_week && n_weeks >= 3) {
    ar_resolution <- "weekly"
  } else {
    ar_resolution <- "monthly"
  }
  res_rank <- c(monthly = 1L, weekly = 2L, daily = 3L)
  pop_cap  <- params$ar_max_resolution[[population_name]] %||% "daily"
  if(res_rank[[ar_resolution]] > res_rank[[pop_cap]]) ar_resolution <- pop_cap
  if(ar_resolution == "daily") {
    P_n <- D;                     pvec <- days$day_index
  } else if(ar_resolution == "weekly") {
    P_n <- n_weeks;               pvec <- days$week_index
  } else {
    P_n <- max(days$month_index); pvec <- days$month_index
  }

  int_cg <- summ$interview |> mutate(fish_count = .data[[est_catch_group]])
  if(use_gear_hrs) {
    int_cg <- int_cg |> filter(!is.na(gear_time_total), gear_time_total > 0)
    h_vec_name <- "gear_time_total"
  } else {
    int_cg <- int_cg |> filter(!is.na(fishing_time_total), fishing_time_total > 0)
    h_vec_name <- "fishing_time_total"
  }
  int_d <- int_cg |> left_join(days |> select(event_date, day_index), by = "event_date") |>
    filter(!is.na(day_index)) |> distinct(interview_id, .keep_all = TRUE)
  intA <- int_d |> filter(!is.na(number_of_gear), number_of_gear > 0, angler_count > 0)

  # Day length (existing logic assumed)
  L_data_vec <- days$L_mu
  L_sigma_vec <- days$L_prior_sigma
  estimate_L_flag <- as.integer(is_shore)

  # Effort obs counts
  n_gear <- if(is_shore) nrow(eff_d) else 0L
  n_trailer <- if(!is_shore) nrow(eff_d) else 0L
  n_effort_obs <- n_gear + n_trailer

  # I/E
  IE_n <- 0L
  ie_match <- tibble()
  if(!is.null(ie_data) && nrow(ie_data) > 0) {
    ie_pop <- if(is_shore) "shore" else "private_boat"
    ie_match <- ie_data |>
      filter(population == ie_pop) |>
      inner_join(days |> select(event_date, day_index), by = "event_date") |>
      filter(!is.na(day_index), ie_crabber_hours > 0)
    IE_n <- nrow(ie_match)
  }

  R_G_empirical <- summ$empirical_R_G
  mu_E_prior <- if(is_shore) log(25) else if(use_gear_hrs) log(20) else log(10)
  mu_C_prior <- if(use_gear_hrs) log(0.1) else log(0.5)

  # --- Covariate matrices ---
  X_E_info <- build_covariate_matrix(effort_features, covariate_df, days$event_date)
  X_C_info <- build_covariate_matrix(cpue_features,   covariate_df, days$event_date)

  stan_data <- list(
    D = D, G = G, S = S,
    P_n = P_n, period = pvec,
    w = days$day_type_num_weekend, holiday = days$day_type_num_holiday,
    O = array(1.0, dim = c(D, S, G)),

    L_data = L_data_vec, estimate_L = estimate_L_flag, L_prior_sigma = L_sigma_vec,
    n_effort_obs = n_effort_obs,

    Gear_n = n_gear,
    day_Gear = if(is_shore) eff_d$day_index else integer(0),
    section_Gear = if(is_shore) rep(1L, nrow(eff_d)) else integer(0),
    Gear_I = if(is_shore) as.integer(eff_d$count_quantity) else integer(0),

    T_n = n_trailer,
    day_T = if(!is_shore) eff_d$day_index else integer(0),
    section_T = if(!is_shore) rep(1L, nrow(eff_d)) else integer(0),
    T_I = if(!is_shore) as.integer(eff_d$count_quantity) else integer(0),

    Crab_n = 0L, day_Crab = integer(0), section_Crab = integer(0),
    Crab_I = integer(0), p_I_crab = 1.0,

    IntC = nrow(int_d), day_IntC = int_d$day_index, gear_IntC = rep(1L, nrow(int_d)),
    section_IntC = rep(1L, nrow(int_d)),
    c = as.integer(int_d$fish_count),
    h = int_d[[h_vec_name]],

    IntA_gear = if(is_shore) nrow(intA) else 0L,
    Gear_A = if(is_shore) as.integer(intA$number_of_gear) else integer(0),
    A_A_gear = if(is_shore) as.integer(intA$angler_count) else integer(0),

    IntA_trailer = if(!is_shore) nrow(intA) else 0L,
    T_A_int = if(!is_shore) rep(1L, nrow(intA)) else integer(0),
    A_A_trailer = if(!is_shore) as.integer(intA$angler_count) else integer(0),

    IE_n = IE_n,
    day_IE = if(IE_n > 0) ie_match$day_index else integer(0),
    section_IE = if(IE_n > 0) rep(1L, IE_n) else integer(0),
    IE_crabber_hours = if(IE_n > 0) ie_match$ie_crabber_hours else numeric(0),

    # Existing hyperparameters
    value_cauchyDF_sigma_eps_E = 1, value_cauchyDF_sigma_r_E = 1,
    value_cauchyDF_sigma_eps_C = 1, value_cauchyDF_sigma_r_C = 1,
    value_betashape_phi_E_scaled = 2, value_betashape_phi_C_scaled = 2,
    value_normal_sigma_B1 = 1, value_normal_sigma_B2 = 1,
    value_normal_sigma_B1_C = 1,
    value_normal_mu_mu_C = mu_C_prior, value_normal_sigma_mu_C = 2,
    value_normal_mu_mu_E = mu_E_prior, value_normal_sigma_mu_E = 2,
    value_cauchyDF_sigma_mu_C = 1, value_cauchyDF_sigma_mu_E = 1,
    R_G_prior_mu = R_G_empirical, R_G_prior_sigma = 0.3,
    R_T_alpha = 5.0, R_T_beta = 1.0,

    # Covariate additions
    K_E = ncol(X_E_info$X),
    X_E = X_E_info$X,
    K_C = ncol(X_C_info$X),
    X_C = X_C_info$X,
    prior_sd_gamma = params$cov_prior_sd
  )

  attr(stan_data, "ar_resolution") <- ar_resolution
  attr(stan_data, "effort_features") <- X_E_info$features
  attr(stan_data, "cpue_features") <- X_C_info$features
  attr(stan_data, "feature_scalings") <- list(
    effort = list(means = X_E_info$means, sds = X_E_info$sds),
    cpue   = list(means = X_C_info$means, sds = X_C_info$sds)
  )
  stan_data
}


# ===========================================================================
# 8. PSIS-LOO WITH K-FOLD FALLBACK
# ===========================================================================

bss_loo_compare <- function(fit_baseline, fit_covariates,
                            stan_data_baseline, stan_data_covariates,
                            model_file_baseline, model_file_covariates,
                            cv_folds = 5) {

  extract_loo <- function(fit) {
    ll_arr <- rstan::extract(fit, pars = "log_lik", permuted = FALSE)  # iter x chains x N
    ll <- loo::extract_log_lik(fit, parameter_name = "log_lik", merge_chains = FALSE)
    r_eff <- loo::relative_eff(exp(ll))
    loo::loo(ll, r_eff = r_eff)
  }

  loo_baseline   <- tryCatch(extract_loo(fit_baseline),   error = function(e) {
    cat(sprintf("  LOO baseline failed: %s\n", e$message)); NULL
  })
  loo_covariates <- tryCatch(extract_loo(fit_covariates), error = function(e) {
    cat(sprintf("  LOO covariates failed: %s\n", e$message)); NULL
  })

  if(is.null(loo_baseline) || is.null(loo_covariates)) {
    return(list(method = "failed", comparison = NULL))
  }

  cmp <- loo::loo_compare(loo_baseline, loo_covariates)

  # Pareto-k check
  k_base <- pareto_k_values(loo_baseline)
  k_cov  <- pareto_k_values(loo_covariates)
  k_pct_base <- mean(k_base > params$loo_k_threshold, na.rm = TRUE)
  k_pct_cov  <- mean(k_cov > params$loo_k_threshold, na.rm = TRUE)

  cat(sprintf("  PSIS-LOO Pareto-k: baseline %.1f%% >%.1f, covariates %.1f%% >%.1f\n",
              k_pct_base * 100, params$loo_k_threshold,
              k_pct_cov * 100, params$loo_k_threshold))

  method <- "psis_loo"
  if(max(k_pct_base, k_pct_cov) > params$loo_k_pct_trigger) {
    cat("  Pareto-k instability detected; triggering k-fold time-block CV.\n")
    # k-fold CV: hold out contiguous time blocks, refit, compute log predictive density
    cv_result <- kfold_time_block_cv(stan_data_baseline, stan_data_covariates,
                                      model_file_baseline, model_file_covariates,
                                      cv_folds = cv_folds)
    method <- "kfold_tblock"
    return(list(method = method, loo_baseline = loo_baseline,
                loo_covariates = loo_covariates,
                comparison = cmp, kfold = cv_result,
                k_pct_baseline = k_pct_base, k_pct_covariates = k_pct_cov))
  }

  list(method = method, loo_baseline = loo_baseline, loo_covariates = loo_covariates,
       comparison = cmp, kfold = NULL,
       k_pct_baseline = k_pct_base, k_pct_covariates = k_pct_cov)
}

pareto_k_values <- function(loo_obj) {
  if(!is.null(loo_obj$diagnostics$pareto_k)) loo_obj$diagnostics$pareto_k
  else loo::pareto_k_values(loo_obj)
}

kfold_time_block_cv <- function(stan_data_baseline, stan_data_covariates,
                                model_file_baseline, model_file_covariates,
                                cv_folds = 5) {
  # Build time blocks over SAMPLED days (not all days)
  D <- stan_data_baseline$D
  sampled_days <- unique(c(stan_data_baseline$day_Gear,
                           stan_data_baseline$day_T,
                           stan_data_baseline$day_IntC,
                           stan_data_baseline$day_IE))
  sampled_days <- sort(sampled_days)
  if(length(sampled_days) < cv_folds * 5) {
    cat(sprintf("  Only %d sampled days; reducing folds to 3.\n", length(sampled_days)))
    cv_folds <- 3
  }

  # Contiguous time blocks
  block_size <- ceiling(length(sampled_days) / cv_folds)
  fold_ids <- rep(seq_len(cv_folds), each = block_size)[seq_along(sampled_days)]
  day_to_fold <- setNames(fold_ids, as.character(sampled_days))

  lpd_baseline <- numeric(cv_folds)
  lpd_covariates <- numeric(cv_folds)

  for(f in seq_len(cv_folds)) {
    cat(sprintf("    Fold %d of %d...\n", f, cv_folds))
    held_out <- as.integer(names(day_to_fold)[day_to_fold == f])

    # Build training data: drop observations where day_* is in held_out set
    train_base <- mask_stan_data(stan_data_baseline, held_out)
    train_cov  <- mask_stan_data(stan_data_covariates, held_out)
    test_base  <- extract_stan_data(stan_data_baseline, held_out)
    test_cov   <- extract_stan_data(stan_data_covariates, held_out)

    # Refit both
    fit_b <- rstan::stan(file = here("02_stan_models", model_file_baseline),
                         data = train_base, chains = 2, iter = 1500, warmup = 750,
                         cores = 2, refresh = 0, seed = params$bss_seed)
    fit_c <- rstan::stan(file = here("02_stan_models", model_file_covariates),
                         data = train_cov, chains = 2, iter = 1500, warmup = 750,
                         cores = 2, refresh = 0, seed = params$bss_seed)

    # Compute log predictive density on held-out
    lpd_baseline[f]   <- compute_lpd_held_out(fit_b, test_base)
    lpd_covariates[f] <- compute_lpd_held_out(fit_c, test_cov)
  }

  tibble(
    fold = seq_len(cv_folds),
    lpd_baseline = lpd_baseline,
    lpd_covariates = lpd_covariates,
    diff = lpd_covariates - lpd_baseline
  )
}

mask_stan_data <- function(stan_data, held_out_days) {
  # Remove observations whose day index is in held_out_days
  d <- stan_data
  remove_indices <- function(day_vec, other_fields) {
    keep <- !day_vec %in% held_out_days
    list(day_vec = day_vec[keep],
         other = lapply(other_fields, function(x) if(length(x) == length(day_vec)) x[keep] else x))
  }
  if(d$Gear_n > 0) {
    r <- remove_indices(d$day_Gear, list(section = d$section_Gear, Gear_I = d$Gear_I))
    d$day_Gear <- r$day_vec; d$section_Gear <- r$other$section; d$Gear_I <- r$other$Gear_I
    d$Gear_n <- length(d$day_Gear)
  }
  if(d$T_n > 0) {
    r <- remove_indices(d$day_T, list(section = d$section_T, T_I = d$T_I))
    d$day_T <- r$day_vec; d$section_T <- r$other$section; d$T_I <- r$other$T_I
    d$T_n <- length(d$day_T)
  }
  if(d$IntC > 0) {
    r <- remove_indices(d$day_IntC,
                        list(gear = d$gear_IntC, section = d$section_IntC,
                             c = d$c, h = d$h))
    d$day_IntC <- r$day_vec; d$gear_IntC <- r$other$gear
    d$section_IntC <- r$other$section; d$c <- r$other$c; d$h <- r$other$h
    d$IntC <- length(d$day_IntC)
  }
  if(d$IE_n > 0) {
    r <- remove_indices(d$day_IE,
                        list(section = d$section_IE, IE = d$IE_crabber_hours))
    d$day_IE <- r$day_vec; d$section_IE <- r$other$section
    d$IE_crabber_hours <- r$other$IE
    d$IE_n <- length(d$day_IE)
  }
  d$n_effort_obs <- d$Gear_n + d$T_n
  d
}

extract_stan_data <- function(stan_data, held_out_days) {
  # Return only the held-out observations for computing predictive density
  d <- list(held_out_days = held_out_days)
  if(stan_data$Gear_n > 0) {
    keep <- stan_data$day_Gear %in% held_out_days
    d$day_Gear <- stan_data$day_Gear[keep]; d$Gear_I <- stan_data$Gear_I[keep]
  }
  if(stan_data$T_n > 0) {
    keep <- stan_data$day_T %in% held_out_days
    d$day_T <- stan_data$day_T[keep]; d$T_I <- stan_data$T_I[keep]
  }
  if(stan_data$IntC > 0) {
    keep <- stan_data$day_IntC %in% held_out_days
    d$day_IntC <- stan_data$day_IntC[keep]; d$c <- stan_data$c[keep]; d$h <- stan_data$h[keep]
  }
  d
}

compute_lpd_held_out <- function(fit, test_data) {
  # Extract posterior means of lambda_E[d] and lambda_C[d] and L[d], then
  # evaluate Poisson log-likelihood on held-out observations (summing over
  # gear counts, trailer counts, and interview catches).
  draws_E <- rstan::extract(fit, "lambda_E_S")$lambda_E_S  # iter x S x D x G (depends on exact structure)
  draws_C <- rstan::extract(fit, "lambda_C_S")$lambda_C_S
  draws_L <- tryCatch(rstan::extract(fit, "L")$L, error = function(e) NULL)

  n_iter <- dim(draws_E)[1]

  # Reshape: expect [iter, D] in scalar case
  if(length(dim(draws_E)) == 4) draws_E <- draws_E[, 1, , 1, drop = TRUE]
  if(length(dim(draws_C)) == 4) draws_C <- draws_C[, 1, , 1, drop = TRUE]

  total_lpd <- 0
  # Gear counts
  if(!is.null(test_data$day_Gear) && length(test_data$day_Gear) > 0) {
    for(i in seq_along(test_data$day_Gear)) {
      d <- test_data$day_Gear[i]; y <- test_data$Gear_I[i]
      lp <- dpois(y, lambda = draws_E[, d], log = TRUE)
      total_lpd <- total_lpd + log_mean_exp(lp)
    }
  }
  if(!is.null(test_data$day_T) && length(test_data$day_T) > 0) {
    for(i in seq_along(test_data$day_T)) {
      d <- test_data$day_T[i]; y <- test_data$T_I[i]
      lp <- dpois(y, lambda = draws_E[, d], log = TRUE)
      total_lpd <- total_lpd + log_mean_exp(lp)
    }
  }
  if(!is.null(test_data$day_IntC) && length(test_data$day_IntC) > 0) {
    for(i in seq_along(test_data$day_IntC)) {
      d <- test_data$day_IntC[i]; y <- test_data$c[i]; h <- test_data$h[i]
      lp <- dpois(y, lambda = draws_C[, d] * h, log = TRUE)
      total_lpd <- total_lpd + log_mean_exp(lp)
    }
  }
  total_lpd
}

log_mean_exp <- function(x) {
  m <- max(x)
  m + log(mean(exp(x - m)))
}


# ===========================================================================
# 8.5 BSS CONVERGENCE CHECK (ported from main RMD v6.1/v6.4 gate)
# Unlike the main model, the module does not fall back to PE; it records
# convergence per fit so an unreliable LOO comparison can be flagged. A fit
# failing this check has an unreliable elpd, so its comparison is suspect.
# ===========================================================================

bss_convergence_check <- function(fit, label, role, max_divergences) {
  sp    <- rstan::get_sampler_params(fit, inc_warmup = FALSE)
  n_div <- sum(sapply(sp, function(x) sum(x[,"divergent__"])))
  max_td <- fit@stan_args[[1]]$control$max_treedepth
  n_td  <- sum(sapply(sp, function(x) sum(x[,"treedepth__"] >= max_td)))
  n_tot <- sum(sapply(sp, nrow))
  s <- summary(fit, pars = c("C_expected_sum", "E_sum"))$summary
  rhat_C <- s["C_expected_sum","Rhat"]; rhat_E <- s["E_sum","Rhat"]
  neff_C <- s["C_expected_sum","n_eff"]; neff_E <- s["E_sum","n_eff"]
  pass_rhat <- max(rhat_C, rhat_E, na.rm = TRUE) < 1.01     # v6.4
  pass_neff <- min(neff_C, neff_E, na.rm = TRUE) > 400
  pass_div  <- (n_div <= max_divergences)                   # v6.1
  pass <- pass_rhat & pass_neff & pass_div
  if(n_tot > 0 && n_td / n_tot > 0.05)
    cat(sprintf("    WARNING: %s [%s] %.1f%% treedepth exceedances\n",
                label, role, n_td / n_tot * 100))
  if(n_div > 0)
    cat(sprintf("    WARNING: %s [%s] %d divergent transitions\n", label, role, n_div))
  tibble(fit = label, role = role,
         divergences = n_div, treedepth_pct = round(n_td / max(n_tot, 1) * 100, 1),
         C_rhat = round(rhat_C, 4), E_rhat = round(rhat_E, 4),
         C_neff = round(neff_C), E_neff = round(neff_E),
         pass_convergence = pass)
}


# ===========================================================================
# 9. MAIN EXECUTION
# ===========================================================================

cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n STEP 1: LOAD CORE DATA FROM MAIN MODEL PIPELINE\n",
    strrep("=", 60), "\n")

# The main RMD's fetch_crab_data() function must be in scope, either through
# a source() call above or by running the main RMD first. For portability,
# we expect `dwg` and `ie_data` to exist in the workspace. If not, direct
# the user to run the main RMD first.
if(!exists("dwg")) stop("Object 'dwg' not found. Run BSS-GH-pooled-CPUE-model.Rmd first ",
                        "or source fetch_crab_data() manually before running this module.")
if(!exists("ie_data")) ie_data <- tibble()

# ---- Build per-day calendar from main model's prep_days ---------------------
days_full <- prep_days_crab(
  params$est_date_start, params$est_date_end,
  params,
  L_eff_model = if(exists("L_eff_model")) L_eff_model else NULL
)


cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n STEP 2: FETCH WEATHER & TIDE\n",
    strrep("=", 60), "\n")

tide_res <- fetch_tide_with_fallback(params$tide_stations,
                                     params$est_date_start, params$est_date_end,
                                     cache_dir = cache_dir,
                                     force_refetch = params$force_refetch)
cat("  Tide station log:\n"); print(tide_res$station_log)
write.csv(tide_res$station_log, file.path(output_dir, "tide_station_log.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

buoy_res <- fetch_buoy_with_fallback(params$ndbc_buoys,
                                     params$est_date_start, params$est_date_end,
                                     cache_dir = cache_dir,
                                     force_refetch = params$force_refetch)
cat("  NDBC buoy log:\n"); print(buoy_res$station_log)
write.csv(buoy_res$station_log, file.path(output_dir, "ndbc_station_log.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

asos_res <- fetch_asos_with_fallback(params$asos_stations, params$gsod_stations,
                                     params$est_date_start, params$est_date_end,
                                     cache_dir = cache_dir,
                                     force_refetch = params$force_refetch)
cat("  ASOS station log:\n"); print(asos_res$station_log)
write.csv(asos_res$station_log, file.path(output_dir, "asos_station_log.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)


cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n STEP 3: BUILD DAILY COVARIATES\n",
    strrep("=", 60), "\n")

sun_cal <- build_sun_calendar(days_full$event_date,
                              params$centroid_lat, params$centroid_lon,
                              params$local_tz)

tide_daily <- compute_tide_daily_features(tide_res$obs, sun_cal)
weather_daily <- compute_weather_daily_features(buoy_res$obs,
                                                asos_res$hourly,
                                                asos_res$daily_gsod,
                                                sun_cal)

int_tide_feats <- compute_interview_tide_features(dwg$interview, tide_res$obs)
int_tide_daily <- if(!is.null(int_tide_feats$daily)) int_tide_feats$daily else tibble()

covariate_df <- tide_daily |>
  left_join(weather_daily, by = "event_date") |>
  left_join(int_tide_daily, by = "event_date") |>
  left_join(days_full |> select(event_date, day_type), by = "event_date")

write.csv(covariate_df, file.path(output_dir, "daily_covariates.csv"), row.names = FALSE)


cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n STEP 4: BOAT DEPARTURE CLUSTERING TEST\n",
    strrep("=", 60), "\n")

if(!is.null(int_tide_feats$per_interview)) {
  dep_test <- departure_clustering_test(int_tide_feats$per_interview,
                                         tide_res$obs, sun_cal)
  if(!is.null(dep_test$test)) {
    print(dep_test)
    write.csv(dep_test, file.path(output_dir, "boat_departure_clustering_test.csv"),
              row.names = FALSE)

    # Robustness: coarsen departure times to the hour and recompute
    per_int_coarse <- int_tide_feats$per_interview |>
      mutate(depart_posix = lubridate::floor_date(depart_posix, unit = "hour"))
    # Refind tide phase at coarsened time
    tide_sorted <- tide_res$obs |> arrange(t)
    idx <- findInterval(as.numeric(per_int_coarse$depart_posix),
                        as.numeric(tide_sorted$t))
    idx <- pmax(pmin(idx, nrow(tide_sorted) - 1), 1)
    per_int_coarse$tide_rising_at_depart <- tide_sorted$v[idx + 1] > tide_sorted$v[idx]
    dep_test_coarse <- departure_clustering_test(per_int_coarse, tide_res$obs, sun_cal)
    cat("  Robustness (times rounded to hour):\n")
    print(dep_test_coarse)
    write.csv(dep_test_coarse,
              file.path(output_dir, "boat_departure_clustering_test_hourly_robustness.csv"),
              row.names = FALSE)
  }
}


cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n STEP 5: LAYER B GAM SCREEN\n",
    strrep("=", 60), "\n")

# Build daily effort aggregates per population for the GAM screen
shore_effort_daily <- dwg$shore_effort |>
  filter(count_sequence <= params$bss_max_count_seq) |>
  group_by(event_date) |>
  summarise(mean_count = mean(count_quantity), .groups = "drop")
boat_effort_daily <- dwg$boat_effort |>
  filter(count_sequence <= params$bss_max_count_seq) |>
  group_by(event_date) |>
  summarise(mean_count = mean(count_quantity), .groups = "drop")

gam_shore_eff <- gam_screen_effort(shore_effort_daily, covariate_df, "shore")
gam_boat_eff  <- gam_screen_effort(boat_effort_daily, covariate_df, "private_boat")
gam_shore_cpue <- gam_screen_cpue(
  dwg$interview |> filter(population == "shore"), covariate_df, "shore"
)
gam_boat_cpue  <- gam_screen_cpue(
  dwg$interview |> filter(population == "private_boat"), covariate_df, "private_boat"
)

# Collect selected features per population
selected_features <- list(
  shore = list(effort = gam_shore_eff$selected, cpue = gam_shore_cpue$selected),
  private_boat = list(effort = gam_boat_eff$selected, cpue = gam_boat_cpue$selected)
)

# Write GAM summaries
for(nm in c("shore_eff","boat_eff","shore_cpue","boat_cpue")) {
  obj_name <- paste0("gam_", nm)
  obj <- get(obj_name)
  if(!is.null(obj$summary_smooth)) {
    write.csv(obj$summary_smooth,
              file.path(output_dir, sprintf("gam_%s_smooths.csv", nm)),
              row.names = FALSE)
  }
}

selected_df <- tibble(
  population = c("shore","shore","private_boat","private_boat"),
  component = c("effort","cpue","effort","cpue"),
  features = c(paste(selected_features$shore$effort, collapse = ";"),
               paste(selected_features$shore$cpue, collapse = ";"),
               paste(selected_features$private_boat$effort, collapse = ";"),
               paste(selected_features$private_boat$cpue, collapse = ";"))
)
write.csv(selected_df, file.path(output_dir, "layer_b_selected_features.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)
cat("\n  Selected features per population:\n"); print(selected_df)


cat("\n", strrep("=", 60),
    "\n STEP 6: BSS BASELINE + AUGMENTED FITS (for comparison)\n",
    strrep("=", 60), "\n")

# This step fits both baseline and augmented BSS models in parallel for
# each (population × sub-season × catch group) combination, then computes
# LOO-PSIS with k-fold fallback. Assumes prep_population_summary() from
# main RMD is available.

bss_baseline_all <- list()
bss_covariate_all <- list()
model_comparisons <- list()
bss_convergence_all <- list()

for(cg in "Dungeness_Kept") {
  for(pop in c("shore","private_boat")) {
    for(ss in list(subseason_1, subseason_2)) {
      label <- paste0(pop, "_", ss$name, "_", cg)
```
