```{r}
      cat(sprintf("\n--- %s ---\n", label))

      days_ss <- prep_days_crab(ss$start, ss$end, params,
                                 L_eff_model = if(exists("L_eff_model")) L_eff_model else NULL)
      summ_ss <- prep_population_summary(dwg, pop, ss$start, ss$end, params)

      n_int <- nrow(summ_ss$interview)
      n_eff <- nrow(summ_ss$effort_index)
      if(n_eff < 5 || n_int < params$bss_min_interviews) {
        cat("  Insufficient data; skipping.\n")
        next
      }

      # Effort and CPUE features for this pop (apply to BOTH subseasons, but
      # note that sub-season 1 is ring-net-only and sub-season 2 is all-gear.
      # Biology may differ; optionally screen per sub-season.)
      # v0.2.3: wrap the per-component fit + LOO so one component that fails (a
      # divergent augmented fit, a LOO/k-fold error, an out-of-memory) logs and
      # the module continues to the next component, the convergence report, and
      # the diagnostics, instead of aborting the whole run.
      tryCatch({
      eff_feat <- selected_features[[pop]]$effort
      cpue_feat <- selected_features[[pop]]$cpue

      bss_data_cov <- prep_bss_crab_augmented(days_ss, summ_ss, cg, params, pop,
                                               ie_data, covariate_df,
                                               eff_feat, cpue_feat)

      # Baseline uses K=0 covariates (same model file, just empty matrices).
      # If your production crab_bss_pooled.stan does NOT accept K_E, K_C, switch
      # this to the original file. The augmented Stan collapses to baseline
      # when K_E = K_C = 0.
      bss_data_base <- bss_data_cov
      bss_data_base$K_E <- 0L
      bss_data_base$K_C <- 0L
      bss_data_base$X_E <- matrix(0, nrow = bss_data_cov$D, ncol = 0)
      bss_data_base$X_C <- matrix(0, nrow = bss_data_cov$D, ncol = 0)

      # v6.2 per-fit sampler tuning (mirrors the main RMD). Both the baseline
      # and covariate fits for a given pop x sub-season use the same settings.
      is_shore   <- (pop == "shore");        is_boat    <- (pop == "private_boat")
      is_allgear <- (ss$name == "all_gear"); is_ringnet <- (ss$name == "ring_net_only")
      if(is_shore && is_allgear) {
        fit_iter <- params$bss_iter_shore_allgear; fit_warmup <- params$bss_warmup_shore_allgear
        fit_treedep <- params$bss_treedepth_shore_allgear %||% params$bss_max_treedepth_default
        fit_delta   <- params$bss_delta_shore_allgear     %||% params$bss_adapt_delta
      } else if(is_boat && is_allgear) {
        fit_iter <- params$bss_iter_boat_allgear   %||% params$bss_iter_default
        fit_warmup <- params$bss_warmup_boat_allgear %||% params$bss_warmup_default
        fit_treedep <- params$bss_treedepth_boat_allgear %||% params$bss_max_treedepth_default
        fit_delta   <- params$bss_delta_boat_allgear     %||% params$bss_adapt_delta
      } else if(is_ringnet) {
        fit_iter <- params$bss_iter_default; fit_warmup <- params$bss_warmup_default
        fit_treedep <- params$bss_treedepth_ringnet %||% params$bss_max_treedepth_default
        fit_delta   <- params$bss_delta_ringnet     %||% params$bss_adapt_delta
      } else {
        fit_iter <- params$bss_iter_default; fit_warmup <- params$bss_warmup_default
        fit_treedep <- params$bss_max_treedepth_default; fit_delta <- params$bss_adapt_delta
      }
      ar_res <- attr(bss_data_cov, "ar_resolution") %||% NA_character_

      cat(sprintf("  Baseline (K_E=0, K_C=0): iter=%d warmup=%d treedepth=%d delta=%.2f AR=%s\n",
                  fit_iter, fit_warmup, fit_treedep, fit_delta, ar_res))
      fit_base <- rstan::stan(
        file = here("02_stan_models", params$bss_model_file_covariates),
        data = bss_data_base,
        chains = params$bss_chains, iter = fit_iter, warmup = fit_warmup,
        control = list(adapt_delta = fit_delta, max_treedepth = fit_treedep),
        seed = params$bss_seed,
        cores = params$bss_cores, refresh = 0
      )

      cat(sprintf("  Covariates (K_E=%d, K_C=%d): iter=%d warmup=%d treedepth=%d delta=%.2f\n",
                  bss_data_cov$K_E, bss_data_cov$K_C, fit_iter, fit_warmup, fit_treedep, fit_delta))
      fit_cov <- rstan::stan(
        file = here("02_stan_models", params$bss_model_file_covariates),
        data = bss_data_cov,
        chains = params$bss_chains, iter = fit_iter, warmup = fit_warmup,
        control = list(adapt_delta = fit_delta, max_treedepth = fit_treedep),
        seed = params$bss_seed,
        cores = params$bss_cores, refresh = 0
      )

      # v6.1/v6.4 convergence diagnostics. The module does not fall back to PE;
      # it records convergence so an unreliable LOO comparison can be flagged.
      conv_base <- bss_convergence_check(fit_base, label, "baseline",   params$max_divergences)
      conv_cov  <- bss_convergence_check(fit_cov,  label, "covariates", params$max_divergences)
      conv_base$ar_resolution <- ar_res; conv_cov$ar_resolution <- ar_res
      bss_convergence_all[[label]] <- bind_rows(conv_base, conv_cov)
      if(!conv_base$pass_convergence || !conv_cov$pass_convergence) {
        cat(sprintf("  CAUTION: %s did not pass the convergence gate (baseline=%s, covariates=%s); its LOO comparison may be unreliable.\n",
                    label, conv_base$pass_convergence, conv_cov$pass_convergence))
      }

      bss_baseline_all[[label]]  <- list(fit = fit_base, stan_data = bss_data_base)
      bss_covariate_all[[label]] <- list(fit = fit_cov, stan_data = bss_data_cov,
                                          eff_features = attr(bss_data_cov, "effort_features"),
                                          cpue_features = attr(bss_data_cov, "cpue_features"))

      cmp <- bss_loo_compare(fit_base, fit_cov, bss_data_base, bss_data_cov,
                              params$bss_model_file_covariates,
                              params$bss_model_file_covariates,
                              cv_folds = params$cv_folds)
      model_comparisons[[label]] <- cmp

      if(!is.null(cmp$comparison)) {
        write.csv(as.data.frame(cmp$comparison),
                  file.path(output_dir, sprintf("loo_comparison_%s.csv", label)),
                  row.names = TRUE)
      }
      if(!is.null(cmp$kfold)) {
        write.csv(cmp$kfold,
                  file.path(output_dir, sprintf("kfold_cv_%s.csv", label)),
                  row.names = FALSE)
      }

      # Pareto-k details
      k_b <- pareto_k_values(cmp$loo_baseline)
      k_c <- pareto_k_values(cmp$loo_covariates)
      write.csv(tibble(obs = seq_along(k_b), k_baseline = k_b, k_covariates = k_c),
                file.path(output_dir, sprintf("pareto_k_%s.csv", label)),
                row.names = FALSE)
      }, error = function(e) {
        cat(sprintf("  ERROR on %s: %s\n  Skipping this component; continuing.\n",
                    label, conditionMessage(e)))
      })
    }
  }
}


# Convergence report across all fits (baseline + covariates for each component).
# Both rows of a component must pass for its LOO comparison to be trustworthy.
if(length(bss_convergence_all) > 0) {
  convergence_df <- bind_rows(bss_convergence_all)
  cat("\n  BSS convergence summary (both fits must pass for a trustworthy LOO):\n")
  print(convergence_df)
  write.csv(convergence_df, file.path(output_dir, "convergence_report.csv"),
            row.names = FALSE)
}


# ---- PER-FIT MODEL DIAGNOSTICS (B1.6) ----
# Structural parameters, divergence localization, and posterior predictive
# calibration for every baseline and covariate fit. write_bss_diagnostics() is
# sourced from 03_R_functions/model_diagnostics.R and is fully tryCatch-wrapped, so
# a diagnostic failure prints a note and never interrupts the run. The augmented
# Stan shares lambda_E_S / lambda_C_S / r_E / r_C with the baseline, so the same
# diagnostics apply to both; outputs use a _baseline / _covariates suffix.
cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n MODEL DIAGNOSTICS\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n")
for(label in names(bss_baseline_all)) {
  e <- bss_baseline_all[[label]]
  if(is.null(e$fit)) next
  cat(sprintf("  %s (baseline):\n", label))
  write_bss_diagnostics(e$fit, e$stan_data, paste0(label, "_baseline"), output_dir)
}
for(label in names(bss_covariate_all)) {
  e <- bss_covariate_all[[label]]
  if(is.null(e$fit)) next
  cat(sprintf("  %s (covariates):\n", label))
  write_bss_diagnostics(e$fit, e$stan_data, paste0(label, "_covariates"), output_dir)
}


cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n STEP 7: INCLUSION DECISIONS\n",
    strrep("=", 60), "\n")

decision_rows <- list()
for(label in names(model_comparisons)) {
  cmp <- model_comparisons[[label]]
  if(is.null(cmp$comparison)) {
    decision_rows[[label]] <- tibble(label = label, elpd_diff = NA, se_diff = NA,
                                      method = cmp$method, include = FALSE,
                                      reason = "comparison_failed")
    next
  }
  # loo_compare returns ordered; row 1 is best, row 2 is elpd_diff to best with SE
  elpd_diff <- cmp$comparison[2, "elpd_diff"]
  se_diff   <- cmp$comparison[2, "se_diff"]

  # If best model is "model2" (covariates), sign is negative for baseline
  # Inspect model order
  best_row <- rownames(cmp$comparison)[1]
  covariates_is_best <- grepl("model2", best_row) || grepl("covariate", best_row)

  include <- (covariates_is_best) && (abs(elpd_diff) > params$cov_elpd_margin_in_se * se_diff)

  # Also require sign consistency for the key gamma parameters (biology check)
  # Extract posterior signs of gamma_E and gamma_C
  sign_check <- TRUE
  sign_notes <- ""
  if(include) {
    fit <- bss_covariate_all[[label]]$fit
    if("gamma_E" %in% fit@model_pars) {
      ge <- rstan::extract(fit, "gamma_E")$gamma_E
      ge_sig <- apply(ge, 2, function(x) mean(x > 0))  # P(gamma > 0)
      gc <- rstan::extract(fit, "gamma_C")$gamma_C
      gc_sig <- apply(gc, 2, function(x) mean(x > 0))
      # Sign-consistent if >80% posterior mass on one side of zero for every feature
      all_sig <- all(ge_sig > 0.8 | ge_sig < 0.2) && all(gc_sig > 0.8 | gc_sig < 0.2)
      sign_check <- all_sig
      sign_notes <- paste(sprintf("P(gamma_E>0):%s", paste(round(ge_sig, 2), collapse = ",")),
                          sprintf("P(gamma_C>0):%s", paste(round(gc_sig, 2), collapse = ",")),
                          sep = "; ")
    }
  }

  include_final <- include && sign_check
  reason <- if(!include) sprintf("elpd_diff=%.1f, SE=%.1f, margin<%.1fSE",
                                  elpd_diff, se_diff, params$cov_elpd_margin_in_se)
            else if(!sign_check) "sign_inconsistent_gamma"
            else sprintf("elpd_diff=%.1f, SE=%.1f", elpd_diff, se_diff)

  decision_rows[[label]] <- tibble(
    label = label, elpd_diff = elpd_diff, se_diff = se_diff,
    method = cmp$method, include = include_final, reason = reason,
    sign_notes = sign_notes
  )
}
decision_df <- bind_rows(decision_rows)
print(decision_df)
write.csv(decision_df, file.path(output_dir, "covariate_inclusion_decisions.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)


cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n STEP 8: UNCERTAINTY COMPARISON (CI widths)\n",
    strrep("=", 60), "\n")

ci_width_rows <- list()
for(label in names(bss_covariate_all)) {
  fit_base <- bss_baseline_all[[label]]$fit
  fit_cov  <- bss_covariate_all[[label]]$fit

  # Compare width of C_expected_sum and E_sum 95% CIs on SAMPLED vs UNSAMPLED days
  # Simpler summary: width of overall season total CIs
  summarize_fit <- function(fit) {
    s <- summary(fit, pars = c("C_expected_sum","E_sum"))$summary
    tibble(
      C_med = s["C_expected_sum","50%"],
      C_width95 = s["C_expected_sum","97.5%"] - s["C_expected_sum","2.5%"],
      E_med = s["E_sum","50%"],
      E_width95 = s["E_sum","97.5%"] - s["E_sum","2.5%"]
    )
  }
  b <- summarize_fit(fit_base); c <- summarize_fit(fit_cov)
  ci_width_rows[[label]] <- tibble(
    label = label,
    C_width_baseline = b$C_width95, C_width_covariates = c$C_width95,
    C_width_pct_change = (c$C_width95 - b$C_width95) / b$C_width95 * 100,
    E_width_baseline = b$E_width95, E_width_covariates = c$E_width95,
    E_width_pct_change = (c$E_width95 - b$E_width95) / b$E_width95 * 100
  )
}
ci_width_df <- bind_rows(ci_width_rows)
print(ci_width_df)
write.csv(ci_width_df, file.path(output_dir, "ci_width_comparison.csv"), row.names = FALSE)


cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n STEP 9: FINAL ESTIMATES (selected model per fit)\n",
    strrep("=", 60), "\n")

final_estimates <- list()
for(label in names(bss_covariate_all)) {
  dec <- decision_df |> filter(label == !!label)
  use_covariates <- isTRUE(dec$include)
  chosen <- if(use_covariates) bss_covariate_all[[label]] else bss_baseline_all[[label]]
  fit <- chosen$fit

  s_ce <- summary(fit, pars = "C_expected_sum")$summary
  s_e  <- summary(fit, pars = "E_sum")$summary

  final_estimates[[label]] <- tibble(
    label = label,
    model_used = if(use_covariates) "covariates" else "baseline",
    features = if(use_covariates)
                 paste(c(chosen$eff_features, chosen$cpue_features), collapse = ";")
               else "",
    C_expected_median = s_ce["C_expected_sum","50%"],
    C_expected_lo95   = s_ce["C_expected_sum","2.5%"],
    C_expected_hi95   = s_ce["C_expected_sum","97.5%"],
    E_median = s_e["E_sum","50%"],
    E_lo95   = s_e["E_sum","2.5%"],
    E_hi95   = s_e["E_sum","97.5%"]
  )
}
final_df <- bind_rows(final_estimates)
print(final_df)
write.csv(final_df, file.path(output_dir, "final_bss_estimates_with_covariate_selection.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)


cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n STEP 10: DIAGNOSTIC PLOTS\n",
    strrep("=", 60), "\n")

# --- Covariate effect plots (posterior forest of gamma) ---
gamma_rows <- list()
for(label in names(bss_covariate_all)) {
  fit <- bss_covariate_all[[label]]$fit
  feats_e <- bss_covariate_all[[label]]$eff_features
  feats_c <- bss_covariate_all[[label]]$cpue_features
  if("gamma_E" %in% fit@model_pars && length(feats_e) > 0) {
    ge <- rstan::extract(fit, "gamma_E")$gamma_E
    for(j in seq_along(feats_e)) {
      gamma_rows[[length(gamma_rows) + 1]] <- tibble(
        label = label, component = "effort", feature = feats_e[j],
        median = median(ge[, j]), lo95 = quantile(ge[, j], 0.025),
        hi95 = quantile(ge[, j], 0.975)
      )
    }
  }
  if("gamma_C" %in% fit@model_pars && length(feats_c) > 0) {
    gc <- rstan::extract(fit, "gamma_C")$gamma_C
    for(j in seq_along(feats_c)) {
      gamma_rows[[length(gamma_rows) + 1]] <- tibble(
        label = label, component = "cpue", feature = feats_c[j],
        median = median(gc[, j]), lo95 = quantile(gc[, j], 0.025),
        hi95 = quantile(gc[, j], 0.975)
      )
    }
  }
}
gamma_df <- bind_rows(gamma_rows)
write.csv(gamma_df, file.path(output_dir, "covariate_effects_posterior.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

if(nrow(gamma_df) > 0) {
  p_gamma <- ggplot(gamma_df,
                    aes(x = median, xmin = lo95, xmax = hi95,
                        y = feature, color = component)) +
    geom_pointrange() +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
    facet_wrap(~label, scales = "free_y") +
    labs(title = "Covariate effects on log(lambda) (BSS augmented model)",
         subtitle = "Effects are on standardized covariates (mean 0, SD 1)",
         x = "Posterior gamma (log-scale effect)", y = "", color = "Component") +
    theme_bw()
  ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "covariate_effects_forest.png"),
         p_gamma, width = 12, height = 8)
}


# B1.6: session and version capture for reproducibility (parallels the pooled
# model). Pins package and Stan versions and the seed with each output set.
writeLines(capture.output(sessionInfo()), file.path(output_dir, "session_info.txt"))
cat(sprintf("\nrstan %s; StanHeaders %s; bss_seed %s\n",
            utils::packageVersion("rstan"),
            utils::packageVersion("StanHeaders"),
            params$bss_seed),
    file = file.path(output_dir, "session_info.txt"), append = TRUE)


cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n DONE\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n")
cat(sprintf("Outputs written to: %s\n", output_dir))
cat(paste(" ", list.files(output_dir), collapse = "\n"), "\n")
```
